mission.title: podman-desktop: Podman Desktop fails to download components when behind transparent network proxy (self signed cert error)
mission.description: Podman Desktop fails to download components when behind transparent network proxy (self signed cert error). This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify podman-desktop troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your podman-desktop deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n podman-desktop -l app.kubernetes.io/name=podman-desktop
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=podman-desktop -n podman-desktop --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review podman-desktop configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant podman-desktop configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n podman-desktop -l app.kubernetes.io/name=podman-desktop
kubectl get configmap -n podman-desktop -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=podman-desktop
```
### Bug description

Many corporate users will be beind a transparent proxy. This means https connections will get intercepted and the corporate cert will be inserted.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Podman Desktop fails to download components when behind…
steps.description:
### What does this PR do?

Update the certificates loaded from the Windows store to include both root and intermediate CA certificates

### Screenshot / video of UI

N/A

### What issues does this PR fix or reference?
### How to test this PR?
1. Unzip the zip file in a directory
2. Run podman run -d --rm --name squid -v squid:/etc/squid -p 3128:3128 ubuntu/squid
3. Add rootCA.crt to the Windows Root Certificates store and CA.crt to the Intermediate Certificate store
4. Launch Podman Desktop and

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Podman Desktop fails to download components when… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=podman-desktop -n podman-desktop --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n podman-desktop --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
### What does this PR do?

Update the certificates loaded from the Windows store to include both root and intermediate CA certificates
